title: M1a — Durable payment/order handshake (prove the loop)
description:
## Outcome
A single-box lnrent daemon that proves the full rent loop end to end with a trivial, instant recipe: publish a NIP-99 listing, take a NIP-17 order, reserve capacity, issue a phoenixd invoice, capture the settlement idempotently, provision, deliver credentials over a NIP-17 DM, and run the prepaid-expiry subscription lifecycle (renew / suspend / destroy) with capture-then-refund and crash recovery.

## Why this exists
The order/payment handshake is the product and its riskiest part. M1a de-risks it independent of VM/Incus/Iroh complexity (M1b/M1c) by exercising the whole durable money path against a trivial recipe (a WireGuard peer or a dummy service). If this is correct, everything else is recipes.

## Scope
The handshake control plane only: store actor, phoenixd payment client, Nostr engine (NIP-99 + NIP-17 DM protocol), recipe runner + one trivial recipe, pre-flight + capacity reservation, idempotent capture, reconcile-loop state machine + downtime credit, provision + delivery outbox, capture-then-refund ledger, the CLI<->daemon IPC surface, a minimal CLI buyer, operator identity/config bootstrap, and the end-to-end money-path tests.

## Non-goals
No Incus/VM provisioning, no Iroh/Tor reachability, no security tiers beyond Tier 0 labeling, no per-box key split / operator manifest (single account-0 key), no NWC pull, no web client. Those are M1b/M1c/M5.

## Critical constraints
AI-free control plane (pure Rust, no LLM at runtime). Daemon is the SOLE writer of sqlite (ADR-0001). phoenixd/Fedimint cannot hold invoices, so capture-then-refund (ADR-0003). Durable, crash-safe handshake (ADR-0009).

## Spec refs
SPEC.md v0.18 §5.1, §6 (§6.2/§6.3/§6.5/§6.6), §9.3, §11; ADR-0001, ADR-0003, ADR-0005, ADR-0009; eng-review T1-T14.

## Success Criteria
- The full M1a loop runs on one box with the trivial recipe: list -> order -> reserve -> invoice -> pay -> capture -> provision -> deliver -> renew -> suspend -> destroy.
- All four mandatory money-path tests pass green in CI (see lnrent-7fp.15).
- The daemon can be killed at any handshake step and recovers correctly on restart.
- A buyer who pays always ends in exactly one of: credentials delivered, or funds refunded. Never stranded, never silently charged.

title: Store actor + sqlite layer (sole writer)
description:
## Outcome
One tokio task owns the rusqlite Connection and is the only writer; reconcile, Nostr, payment, and IPC tasks send typed commands over an mpsc channel and await replies. Provides transactional CRUD for all §11 tables and the atomic multi-row transitions the handshake needs (e.g. invoice OPEN->PAID + sub PENDING->PROVISIONING in one txn).

## Why
ADR-0001: the daemon is the sole writer so the AI-free invariant is structural, not a convention, and there are no write races. The actor keeps rusqlite (sync) on one thread.

## Scope
store module, command enum + mpsc, transaction helpers, event_log append on every transition, sqlite WAL mode, the §11 schema (already in store.rs: 9 tables).

## Non-goals
Business logic (capture/reconcile/refund call the store; they live in their own beads).

## Constraints / failure handling
Sole writer; every mutation journaled to event_log; atomic captures use one transaction; WAL so reads never block the writer; no lock held across an await.

## Dependencies
Depends on: injectable clock (lnrent-7fp.2). Unblocks: migrations, reservation, capture, reconcile, provision, refund, IPC, backup, operator-init.

## Verification
Unit: each txn helper + the atomic capture txn (rollback on error). Keep the schema test (9 tables). A test that concurrent command senders serialize.

## Spec refs
ADR-0001; SPEC §11; eng-review T1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 9 §11 tables exist; the schema test passes.
- The capture transaction (invoice OPEN->PAID + sub PENDING->PROVISIONING + event_log append) commits or rolls back atomically (unit test proves rollback on injected error).
- Every write goes through the actor; a concurrency test shows commands serialize with no lost update.
- WAL is enabled; a read does not block a concurrent write.

title: Provision + delivery outbox (crash-safe)
description:
## Outcome
The crash-safe settle->capture->provision->deliver sequence. On PROVISIONING, run the recipe provision hook (idempotent, re-run on restart); on success, in ONE transaction move the subscription -> ACTIVE, set paid_through = now + period, and write an outbox row carrying the provision.ready DM. A sender task drains the outbox, publishes the NIP-17 DM, marks it SENT, and retries until sent. A crash after ACTIVE but before send re-sends on restart, so a paid buyer is never stranded.
## Why
If ACTIVE is committed before the credentials DM is delivered, the buyer paid but may never receive access; relays also drop DMs. The durable outbox guarantees delivery and is the dropped-DM resync answer (codex #6 / ADR-0009).
## Scope
provision invocation via the recipe runner; the atomic ACTIVE+outbox transaction; the outbox sender (drain/publish/retry/mark SENT); restart re-drive of PROVISIONING and unsent outbox.
## Non-goals
Refund on provision failure (refund bead); the Nostr publish primitive (nostr bead).
## Constraints / failure handling
provision hooks idempotent; outbox write is in the same txn as ACTIVE; sender retries with backoff; restart re-runs stuck PROVISIONING and resends unsent outbox rows.
## Dependencies
Depends on: store, recipe runner, nostr, capture. Unblocks: e2e.
## Verification
Integration: kill the daemon between ACTIVE and outbox-send -> the DM is resent on restart; the buyer processes provision.ready idempotently (at-least-once delivery, deduped by message id).
## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §6.6; ADR-0009; eng-review T4.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kill the daemon between sub->ACTIVE and outbox-send: provision.ready is resent on restart (integration).
- provision.ready is delivered at-least-once and deduped by a stable message id (NOT exactly-once network delivery); the buyer processes it idempotently.
- A subscription stuck in PROVISIONING is re-driven on restart.

## Note (codex)
The ACTIVE transaction also CONSUMES the order's reservation (HELD->CONSUMED, lnrent-7fp.7).

title: Capture-then-refund + refund ledger
description:
## Outcome
Capture-then-refund with a durable ledger. On permanent provision failure (after retries) -> REFUND_DUE + a refund_attempt row (dest from order.request, amount, status PENDING). A refunder pays via PaymentBackend::pay (records backend_payment_id), checks status via payment_status, retries; after N failures the subscription stays REFUND_DUE and the operator is alerted (billing.refund DM status=failed). Also handles settled-but-expired (a settlement after invoice expiry -> auto-refund, order not resurrected).
## Why
phoenixd/Fedimint can't hold invoices, so funds are captured before provisioning; if provision fails (or a payment lands on an expired order) the buyer must be made whole without a manual op (ADR-0003). BOLT12-offer/LN-address refunds can fail to an offline payer, so the ledger must retry and never double-pay.
## Scope
the refund_attempt ledger lifecycle; the refunder task (pay/status/retry/alert); executing settled-but-expired refunds (detection/creation owned by lnrent-7fp.8); the billing.refund DM.
## Non-goals
Receiving (phoenixd bead); the capture transition (capture bead).
## Constraints / failure handling
never double-pay (dedupe on backend_payment_id); REFUND_DUE-stuck after N attempts alerts the operator; refund dest collected up front in order.request (BOLT12 offer preferred, LN address fallback).
## Dependencies
Depends on: store, phoenixd, reconcile. Unblocks: e2e.
## Verification
Integration: provision failure -> auto-refund to a test dest; refund send fails -> stays REFUND_DUE + alert, no double-pay on retry; settled-but-expired -> refund not provision.
## Spec refs
SPEC §6.3, §6.4, §6.6; ADR-0003, ADR-0009; eng-review T7; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provision failure -> auto-refund to a test dest; subscription -> REFUNDED (integration).
- Refund send fails -> subscription stays REFUND_DUE + operator alert; a retry does NOT double-pay (dedupe on backend_payment_id).
- Settled-but-expired -> refund_attempt, not provision.

## Note (codex)
Releases the reservation on REFUND_DUE (lnrent-7fp.7). Sends billing.refund (status sent/failed) to the buyer by enqueueing a billing.refund outbox row (delivered by the sender in lnrent-7fp.10).

title: lnrent CLI <-> daemon IPC surface
description:
## Outcome
A local CLI<->daemon IPC surface: a Unix-domain socket the daemon owns, with a small JSON/line command set (status, recipes, subs, and admin: force suspend/resume, inspect a subscription). The lnrent CLI and the Claude skills act ONLY through this; they never touch sqlite directly.
## Why
ADR-0001 says skills act through the lnrent CLI with the daemon as sole writer; the surface was never specified. Without it skills hit sqlite directly and break the invariant (eng-review #2).
## Scope
the UDS server in the daemon (commands routed to the store actor); the lnrent CLI client wired to it (replace the M0 stub prints); read commands + a few admin mutations.
## Non-goals
Remote/networked admin API (forbidden by the VM guidelines); the buyer client (separate bead).
## Constraints / failure handling
socket in the data dir with tight perms; every mutation goes through the store actor (sole writer) and is journaled; no public/networked exposure.
## Dependencies
Depends on: store. Unblocks: e2e (operator-side checks).
## Verification
Unit/integration: lnrent status/recipes/subs return live state via the socket; an admin suspend goes through the store actor and is journaled.
## Spec refs
ADR-0001; SPEC §4.2, §10; eng-review architecture #2.

## Acceptance Criteria
- lnrent status / recipes / subs return live state via the Unix socket (integration).
- An admin suspend routes through the store actor and is journaled to event_log.
- The socket is not network-reachable.

title: Minimal CLI buyer
description:
## Outcome
A minimal CLI buyer that drives the loop end to end: discover a NIP-99 listing, send order.request (with a refund dest + params), receive order.invoice, show the bolt11 to pay, receive provision.ready, and print the delivered credentials. Used to exercise M1a without the web client.
## Why
M1a needs a buyer side to prove list->order->pay->provision->deliver; the web client is M4. This is also the manual test harness for the loop.
## Scope
a small Rust buyer binary (clients/cli) speaking the NIP-17 DM protocol via the Nostr engine primitives; a local nostr key; pay-by-copy bolt11 (no embedded wallet).
## Non-goals
Operator manifest verification (M5); WebLN/NWC; the web client (M4).
## Dependencies
Depends on: nostr engine. Unblocks: e2e.
## Verification
Integration: the buyer completes a full order->pay->receive-creds against a running daemon + local relay + phoenixd mock.
## Spec refs
SPEC §3, §5.1, §14; eng-review T13.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The CLI buyer completes order -> pay -> receive-creds against a running daemon + local relay + phoenixd mock (integration).

title: Daemon state backup
description:
## Outcome
A backup mechanism for the daemon's own sqlite state (subscriptions, invoices, paid_through, reservations, refund ledger, outbox) so the operator's commitments survive box death even though the BIP39 seed is backed up separately.
## Why
ADR-0004 forces seed backup, but losing the sqlite loses who-paid-through-when for a system holding other people's prepaid time (eng-review #5). 
## Scope
a periodic consistent sqlite backup (online backup API or litestream-style), operator-configurable destination; a documented restore path.
## Non-goals
Multi-box replication/HA (M7); seed backup (ADR-0004).
## Dependencies
Depends on: store. P2 (not a ship blocker for the loop, but required before real money).
## Verification
Integration: kill+restore from backup reproduces subscription/paid_through/ledger state.
## Spec refs
eng-review (inferred requirement; SPEC §13 is security, not backup); eng-review T14; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kill + restore from backup reproduces subscription / paid_through / reservation / refund-ledger state (integration).

title: M1a end-to-end loop + money-path tests (ship gate)
description:
## Outcome
The M1a ship gate: an end-to-end test that runs the whole loop on one box against a phoenixd mock/regtest and a local relay with the trivial recipe — publish listing -> order.request -> pre-flight+reserve -> invoice -> pay -> idempotent capture -> provision -> provision.ready delivered -> renew -> suspend -> destroy. PLUS the mandatory money-path tests (these gate ship): idempotent-capture replay (no double-extend), settled-but-expired auto-refund (no silent theft), crash recovery (kill between capture and deliver -> recovered), and downtime credit (operator outage doesn't suspend a buyer unfairly).
## Why
M1a's whole purpose is to prove the handshake is correct. These are money paths: if any is wrong and silent, funds vanish or buyers are stranded. They are non-negotiable (eng-review failure-modes; outside-voice; codex).
## Scope
the e2e harness (daemon + local relay + phoenixd mock + trivial recipe + CLI buyer); the four mandatory money-path tests; CI wiring.
## Non-goals
VM/Incus, Iroh/Tor, tiers (M1b/M1c).
## Constraints
deterministic via the injectable clock; no real funds.
## Dependencies
Depends on: reservation, capture, reconcile, provision+outbox, refund, IPC, buyer. This is the milestone-completion bead.
## Verification
This bead IS the verification. Acceptance signal: the full loop test + all four money-path tests pass green in CI.
## Spec refs
SPEC §6.6, §15 (M1a); ADR-0003/0005/0009; eng-review failure modes + Implementation Tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full loop test green: list -> order -> reserve -> invoice -> pay -> capture -> provision -> deliver -> renew -> suspend -> destroy.
- Money-path 1 (idempotent capture): a duplicate settlement extends paid_through exactly once.
- Money-path 2 (settled-but-expired): a late settlement auto-refunds, no provision, no kept funds.
- Money-path 3 (crash recovery): killing the daemon after capture, mid-provision, and after ACTIVE-before-deliver each recovers correctly.
- Money-path 4 (downtime credit): an operator outage across a renewal window does NOT suspend the buyer on restart.
- All deterministic via TestClock, no real funds, wired into CI.

title: Operator identity + config bootstrap
description:
## Outcome
Bootstrap the operator: from a BIP39 seed derive the account-0 Nostr identity (NIP-06, m/44'/1237'/0'/0/0), persist the operator row (master_pubkey, op_pubkey, box_index=0), and load runtime config — the phoenixd endpoint and the default relay set. Enough for the daemon to sign listings/DMs and receive payments in M1a.

## Why
The handshake cannot run without an operator identity + backend/relay config. M1a uses a single account-0 key (the master/per-box split + operator manifest are M5, ADR-0004). Without this nothing can sign a 30402 or open the phoenixd connection.

## Scope
seed -> account-0 key (NIP-06) via rust-nostr; operator-row persistence through the store actor; load relays + phoenixd endpoint from config / data dir.

## Non-goals
Per-box operational keys + operator manifest (M5); the lnrent-onboard skill UX; seed backup (ADR-0004).

## Constraints / failure handling
single key in M1a (account 0 = master, used directly); seed/key live in the data dir with tight perms, never logged.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Used by the Nostr engine + payment config at wire-up. Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Unit: deterministic derivation from a fixed seed.

## Spec refs
ADR-0004, SPEC §4.6, §11 (operator table).

## Acceptance Criteria
- From a fixed test seed, the account-0 npub is deterministic and matches a NIP-06 derivation vector.
- The operator row is persisted (master_pubkey, op_pubkey, box_index=0) via the store actor.
- The daemon signs and publishes a 30402 listing with the derived key.
- phoenixd endpoint + default relays load from config.

title: Order intake + invoice issuance
description:
## Outcome
The order-intake + invoice-issuance integrator that owns ADR-0009's "the PENDING subscription IS the order." Two paths:
- New order: on order.request, validate params + pre-flight/reserve capacity (lnrent-7fp.7), create the subscription PENDING + an invoice OPEN with a unique external_id in ONE store transaction, call phoenixd create_invoice(external_id), and send order.invoice (or order.error + release the reservation on validation/capacity failure).
- Renewal invoice: at soft_date (driven by reconcile, lnrent-7fp.9) or on renew.request, issue a renewal invoice (new invoice row + create_invoice) and send billing.invoice.

## Why
Launch review: no bead owned the order->invoice orchestration; capture/reservation/payment/nostr each held a piece but "create the PENDING sub + OPEN invoice and send order.invoice" was orphaned. ADR-0009 makes the PENDING sub the durable order that settlement correlates to via external_id.

## Scope
order.request handling; the create-PENDING-sub + OPEN-invoice transaction; phoenixd create_invoice with external_id; sending order.invoice/order.error/billing.invoice via the Nostr transport (lnrent-7fp.5); renewal-invoice issuance.

## Non-goals
Settlement capture (lnrent-7fp.8); provisioning (lnrent-7fp.10); the DM transport primitives (lnrent-7fp.5); deadline firing (lnrent-7fp.9).

## Constraints / failure handling
PENDING sub + OPEN invoice created in one transaction so the order is durable before the invoice is shown; on failure send order.error and release the reservation, leaving no dangling PENDING sub; external_id unique per invoice; duplicate order.request (same request id) is idempotent.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1), phoenixd (lnrent-7fp.4), Nostr transport (lnrent-7fp.5), reservation (lnrent-7fp.7). Unblocks: reconcile renewal (lnrent-7fp.9), e2e (lnrent-7fp.15).

## Verification
Integration over a local relay + phoenixd mock.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §6.2, §6.4, §6.6; ADR-0009.

## Acceptance Criteria
- order.request -> a PENDING subscription + an OPEN invoice (unique external_id) are created in one transaction and order.invoice is sent with the bolt11.
- Invalid params or insufficient capacity -> order.error sent + reservation released; no dangling PENDING sub.
- At soft_date or on renew.request, a renewal invoice is issued and billing.invoice is sent.
- A duplicate order.request (same request id) does not create a second order.

## Ordering / atomicity (codex)
Reserve capacity (lnrent-7fp.7), then create the PENDING sub + OPEN invoice in ONE store transaction, then call phoenixd create_invoice. If create_invoice or persistence fails, RELEASE the reservation and fail the order, leaving no dangling PENDING sub or stuck reservation.

title: Injectable clock for deterministic time
description:
## Outcome
An injectable time source (a Clock trait with now() returning unix seconds) used everywhere deadlines/timestamps are computed, so every time-based transition is deterministic under test.

## Why
The reconcile-loop state machine, invoice expiry, paid_through/soft_date, and downtime credit all key on wall-clock time. Without an injectable clock none of those paths are unit-testable.

## Scope
A Clock trait + a real SystemClock + a TestClock (settable/advanceable). Wire store and reconcile to take a Clock.

## Non-goals
Monotonic-vs-wall subtleties beyond the NTP assumption (a §16 minor risk).

## Dependencies
None (foundational root). Unblocks: store (lnrent-7fp.1), reconcile, and every time-based test.

## Verification
Unit: TestClock advance drives a fake deadline transition without sleeping.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.5; eng-review T11.

## Acceptance Criteria
- TestClock can set and advance time; a reconcile deadline fires under TestClock with no real sleep.
- SystemClock returns wall-clock unix seconds.
- store and reconcile take a Clock by injection (no direct std time calls in those paths).

title: sqlite migrations (user_version)
description:
## Outcome
A minimal schema-version/migration mechanism (sqlite user_version) so the schema can evolve past M0 without a painful first column change.

## Why
store.rs currently applies CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with no versioning; the first real migration would be ad hoc. Codex flagged this.

## Scope
A migrations runner keyed on PRAGMA user_version; M1a is version 1 (the current §11 schema).

## Non-goals
Down-migrations.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: any later schema change.

## Verification
Unit: opening an old (v0) db migrates to v1; opening a current db is a no-op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §11; eng-review T12; codex finding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opening a fresh db sets user_version = 1 and applies the §11 schema.
- Opening a current (v1) db is a no-op.
- Unit test: a simulated v0 db migrates to v1 without data loss.

title: phoenixd PaymentBackend (invoice/watch/pay/status)
description:
## Outcome
A phoenixd-backed PaymentBackend: create_invoice(amount, memo, expiry, external_id) over phoenixd HTTP (externalId binds settlement->order), lookup, pay (returns a backend payment id), payment_status (outbound refund status), and watch() — a push stream of settlements from the phoenixd websocket carrying external_id.

## Why
phoenixd is the v1 receiver (self-custodial, auto-liquidity). The capture loop's timeliness depends on the push settlement stream, not polling (codex). phoenixd cannot hold invoices, hence capture-then-refund downstream (ADR-0003).

## Scope
HTTP client (reqwest) for createinvoice/payinvoice/payoffer/paylnaddress/lookup; the websocket settlement stream mapped to Settlement{invoice_id, external_id, amount_sat}; implements the §6.1 PaymentBackend trait already stubbed in backends.rs.

## Non-goals
Fedimint backend (M3), LND hold-invoice backend (M3). No invoice<->sub correlation logic here (capture bead owns it).

## Constraints / failure handling
ws reconnect must resume the settlement stream; settlements can be redelivered on reconnect (capture must be idempotent). createinvoice sets externalId = the unique per-invoice token.

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane B). Unblocks: capture (lnrent-7fp.8), refund (lnrent-7fp.11).

## Verification
Integration against a phoenixd mock/regtest.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.1, §6.4; ADR-0003, ADR-0009; eng-review T2; codex verify.

## Acceptance Criteria
- create_invoice(.., external_id) returns a bolt11 whose externalId == external_id.
- On settlement, watch() emits Settlement{invoice_id, external_id, amount_sat}.
- A ws reconnect replays a prior settlement (test asserts redelivery).
- pay() returns a non-empty payment id; payment_status(id) reflects sent/failed.

title: Nostr engine: NIP-99 listings + NIP-17 DM protocol
description:
## Outcome
A Nostr engine that (a) publishes NIP-99 classified listings (kind 30402, with an operator tag) for the operator's offers, and (b) speaks the lnrent DM protocol over NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs: order.request, order.invoice, order.error, provision.ready, billing.invoice, billing.notice, billing.refund, renew.request, sub.cancel.

## Why
The marketplace is Nostr-native (NIP-99 discovery + NIP-17 transaction); NIP-90 was rejected as ill-fitting. NIP-17 over public relays has no ordering/ack/delivery guarantee, so the protocol needs message IDs, idempotency, and buyer resync (codex/outside-voice P1).

## Scope
rust-nostr (nostr-sdk) integration; publish/replace 30402 listings; gift-wrap (NIP-59/44) encode/decode of the DM JSON; per-message id + idempotent handling of duplicates; publish/subscribe across several default relays; account-0 key signing (single key in M1a).

## Non-goals
Operator manifest / per-box key verification (M5); web client (M4). The durable outbox SENDER lives in the provision+outbox bead (lnrent-7fp.10); this bead provides the publish/subscribe primitives it uses.

## Constraints / failure handling
Relays drop events: publish to multiple relays, dedupe inbound by message id, support buyer resync (renew.request prompts redelivery). Do not trust gift-wrap created_at for expiry (use payload fields).

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane C). Unblocks: provision+outbox (lnrent-7fp.10), CLI buyer (lnrent-7fp.13).

## Verification
Integration over a local relay.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §5.2, §5.3; eng-review T5; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A NIP-99 (30402) listing publishes and is fetchable from a local relay, carrying the operator tag.
- order.request -> order.invoice -> provision.ready round-trips over NIP-17 on a local relay.
- A duplicate inbound DM (same message id) is processed exactly once.
- A dropped order.invoice is recovered by the buyer re-sending order.request (idempotent by order/request id); renew.request covers only renewal resync.

## Note (codex)
This bead owns the NIP-17/NIP-99 TRANSPORT primitives (gift-wrap encode/decode, publish/subscribe across relays, message-id dedupe). The order/invoice business orchestration is owned by lnrent-7fp.17.

title: Recipe runner + trivial M1a recipe
description:
## Outcome
A hook runner that executes a recipe's lifecycle hooks (provision/suspend/resume/destroy/healthcheck) as child processes with: a timeout (kill on expiry), bounded stdout/stderr capture, JSON on stdin (subscription+instance+validated params+host facts), JSON result on stdout, run OFF the reconcile loop. Plus ONE trivial instant recipe for M1a (a WireGuard peer or a dummy 'echo creds' service) so the handshake has something to provision.

## Why
A hung/runaway provision must not wedge the reconcile loop (codex/eng-review). Recipes are trusted code (ADR-0002) but still fallible. M1a needs a fast, reliable recipe to prove the loop without VM complexity.

## Scope
process spawn with timeout + output cap + JSON stdin/stdout; Recipe::validate() (hooks exist+executable, backend known, os supported); the trivial recipe dir + hooks.

## Non-goals
Incus/VM recipe (M1b); third-party recipe install (never, ADR-0002).

## Constraints / failure handling
timeout -> kill + treat as provision failure -> refund path; secrets via stdin JSON, not argv/env; idempotent hooks (re-run safe) for crash recovery.

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane E). Unblocks: provision+outbox (lnrent-7fp.10).

## Verification
Unit + a smoke provision.

## Spec refs
SPEC §7, §7.2, §8.1; ADR-0002; eng-review T8, T10.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A hook exceeding its timeout is killed and surfaces as provision failure (unit).
- Oversized stdout/stderr is truncated to the cap, not unbounded.
- Nonzero exit surfaces as failure; Recipe::validate() rejects a recipe with a missing/non-executable hook, unknown backend, or unsupported OS.
- The trivial recipe provisions and returns a delivery-payload JSON on stdout.

title: Pre-flight + capacity reservation
description:
## Outcome
Order-time capacity reservation plus order-param validation. At pre-flight (before the invoice is issued) the daemon validates the order's params against the recipe [[params]], checks available >= requested, and atomically (via the store actor) creates a reservation HELD for the order with TTL = invoice expiry. available = host budget - (active Instances + live reservations). Reservation released on expiry/refund, stays HELD through PROVISIONING, becomes CONSUMED at ACTIVE.

## Why
Pre-flight without reservation lets two concurrent orders race the last slot into capture-then-refund — on a one-box host the scarce resource (RAM/disk/ports) is the second sale, not an edge case (outside-voice P1). Params must be validated before money moves.

## Scope
operator-configured host budget; param validation against the recipe manifest; the reservation table lifecycle (HELD/CONSUMED/RELEASED); pre-flight check + reserve in one store transaction.

## Non-goals
Runtime per-Instance cgroup limits (the compute backend enforces those in M1b).

## Constraints / failure handling
reserve is atomic with the availability check (no TOCTOU); reservation stays HELD across the whole PROVISIONING window so a concurrent order can't reuse the slot mid-provision.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Unit (concurrent reservation) + integration.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.4, §9.3, §7.1; eng-review T9; outside-voice/codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two concurrent orders for the last slot: exactly one reservation is HELD, the other rejected before any invoice (no TOCTOU).
- Order params are validated against the recipe [[params]] at pre-flight; invalid params reject before invoice.
- Reservation is RELEASED on invoice expiry, stays HELD through PROVISIONING, and becomes CONSUMED at ACTIVE.

title: Idempotent settlement capture
description:
## Outcome
Idempotent handling of ALL incoming settlements. On a Settlement, look up the invoice by external_id (UNIQUE), then UPDATE invoice SET status='PAID', settled_at=? WHERE id=? AND status='OPEN' in one transaction with the resulting subscription move:
- first invoice (sub PENDING) -> PROVISIONING (the capture);
- renewal invoice (sub ACTIVE) -> extend paid_through by period (early renewals stack);
- renewal while SUSPENDED (within retention) -> resume + extend paid_through.
A replayed settlement (ws reconnect) affects 0 rows and is a no-op, so paid_through can never double-extend.

## Why
phoenixd ws reconnect replays settlements; the event_log alone prevents double-running a hook, not double-counting a payment. The idempotency key is the invoice status guard, not the transition (codex #3/#5). Renewal and first payment share the same settlement machinery.

## Scope
settlement -> invoice (external_id) -> subscription mapping; the atomic status-guard transaction for first capture, renewal extension, and resume-on-late-renewal.

## Non-goals
Running provision (lnrent-7fp.10); refund execution (lnrent-7fp.11); deadline firing (lnrent-7fp.9 reconcile).

## Constraints / failure handling
external_id UNIQUE per invoice; one transaction per settlement; settlement for an already-PAID invoice is a no-op; settlement for an EXPIRED invoice routes to refund (settled-but-expired), not provisioning.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1), phoenixd (lnrent-7fp.4). Unblocks: provision+outbox, refund, e2e.

## Verification
Unit + integration with duplicate and renewal settlements.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.2, §6.3, §6.6; ADR-0009; eng-review T3; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A duplicate Settlement for the same invoice extends paid_through exactly once (idempotent; unit + integration).
- A first-invoice settlement moves PENDING -> PROVISIONING; a renewal settlement extends paid_through; a renewal while SUSPENDED (in retention) resumes + extends.
- A Settlement for an EXPIRED invoice produces a refund_attempt, never a provision.
- UNIQUE(external_id) holds: a second insert with the same external_id fails.

## Settled-but-expired ownership (codex)
This bead only DETECTS a settlement on an EXPIRED invoice and ROUTES it: it creates sub REFUND_DUE + a refund_attempt (PENDING). The refund EXECUTION (pay/retry/status/alert) is owned by lnrent-7fp.11.

title: Reconcile loop + state machine + downtime credit
description:
## Outcome
A single periodic reconcile loop that advances subscriptions: it scans rows whose next_deadline <= now and fires the due transition (remind at soft_date, suspend at paid_through, destroy at retention end), recomputing next_deadline. Implements the full SubState machine (PENDING/PROVISIONING/ACTIVE/SUSPENDED/TERMINATED/EXPIRED/CANCELLED/REFUND_DUE/REFUNDED). Persists a heartbeat and, on restart, CREDITS operator downtime: any renewal/suspend deadline that fell in the downtime window is shifted by the outage and its reminder re-sent.
## Why
The daemon's core job is enforcing the prepaid-expiry lifecycle over time (ADR-0005). Transitions must be idempotent + journaled so a crash mid-hook can't double-run or wedge. Downtime credit (ADR-0005 revision) means a buyer is never suspended for the operator's outage (home-lab boxes reboot).
## Scope
the reconcile tick; all state transitions per §6.3; renewal extends paid_through (early stacks); heartbeat persist + downtime-credit deadline shift; next_deadline cursor.
## Non-goals
The capture (own bead), provision/deliver (own bead), refund execution (own bead) — reconcile triggers them via the store/commands.
## Constraints / failure handling
absolute wall-clock deadlines; idempotent transitions journaled to event_log; downtime credit on restart; reminders best-effort (buyer can renew.request on demand).
## Dependencies
Depends on: store, clock. Unblocks: refund, e2e.
## Verification
Unit (TestClock): each transition fires at its deadline; downtime-credit shifts a suspend that fell in the outage window and is NOT applied otherwise; missed deadlines fire on restart.
## Spec refs
SPEC §6.2, §6.3, §6.5; ADR-0005 (+ revision); eng-review T6.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each transition fires at its deadline under TestClock (soft_date reminder, suspend at paid_through, destroy at retention).
- Downtime credit: a suspend whose deadline fell in the recorded outage window is shifted by the outage length; a suspend OUTSIDE the window is NOT shifted.
- Deadlines missed while the daemon was down fire on restart.

## Execution and messaging (codex)
This bead owns the state transitions AND their side effects: it fires the suspend/destroy hooks via the recipe runner (lnrent-7fp.6) and sends billing.notice + triggers the soft_date renewal invoice via the order-intake issuance path (lnrent-7fp.17) by ENQUEUEING outbox rows (store writes; the durable sender in lnrent-7fp.10 delivers them, exercised by the e2e). It RELEASES the reservation on TERMINATED (lnrent-7fp.7).
